---
title: "Animated_Flow_Twitter_Map"
author: "Ben Vales"
date: "2026-04-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
#loading packages
library(mapdeck)
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(h3r)
library(h3jsr)
library(readr)
library(sf)
library(scales)
library(usethis)
library(htmlwidgets)
```

```{r}


#load nodes and edges from base graph
nodes <- read_csv(here("data/derived_data/nodes_and_edge_data/nyc_nodes.csv"))
edges <- read_csv(here("data/derived_data/nodes_and_edge_data/nyc_edges.csv"))

#load nyc boundaries shapefiles
nyc_brgh <-st_read(here("data/raw_data/mapping_data/nybb_25d/nybb.shp"))
nyc_h3 <-st_read(here("data/raw_data/mapping_data/nynta2020_25d/nynta2020.shp"))
#transform for mapping later
nyc_brgh <- st_transform(nyc_brgh, crs = 4326)
nyc_h3 <- st_transform(nyc_brgh, crs = 4326)

```



```{r}
#Joins node coordinates onto the edge table so each edge row carries both its origin and destination lat/lon — the format mapdeck expects for arcs.
edges <- edges %>%
  select(from, to, count) %>%   #Drops unused columns; retain only the origin/destination cell,and the flow count between
  left_join(nodes,by = c("from" = "cell_name")) %>% 
  #Left-join nodes on the origin cell ("from") to attach origin coords.
  rename(from_lat = lat, from_lon = lon) %>% #rename coulums to from_lat/from_long

  left_join(nodes, by = c("to" = "cell_name")) %>%
  rename(to_lat = lat, to_lon = lon) #Left-join nodes again on the destination cell ("to") for destination coords.
```


```{r}
#Heat map

# Aggregates total inbound flow per destination cell for mapping
node_heat <- edges %>%
  group_by(to) %>%                              #group by destination cell 
  summarise(total_count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% # summerize all incoming flow counts per cell;
#na.rm = TRUE ignores missing values
  
# attach lat/lon from nodes table so each destination cell has coordinates for mapping
  left_join(nodes, by = c("to" = "cell_name"))      

# Build heatmap
nyc_heatmap <- mapdeck(
  token = Sys.getenv("MAPBOX_TOKEN"),                    #Loads Mapbox API token
  style = mapdeck_style('dark'),                         #dark basemap style
  pitch = 0                                              #flat (top-down) view
) %>%
  add_heatmap(
    data = node_heat,             #inbound aggregated data
    lat = "lat",                 #latitude column from nodes
    lon = "lon",                #longitude column from nodes
    weight = "total_count",        # ntensity driven by total data
    radius_pixels = 30,                  #how far each point "bleeds" — larger = smoother
    intensity = 1,                  #global multiplier on all weight values
    threshold = 0.05                #hides points below 5% of max weight (removes noise)
  ) %>% 
  add_polygon(
    data = nyc_brgh,
    fill_opacity = 0,          # outline only
    stroke_colour = "#FFFFFF", # white border
    stroke_width = 100,         # adjust thickness as needed
    tooltip = "BoroName"       # hover to see borough name
  )
nyc_heatmap
```

```{r}
#Arc-Map 

#Filter Logic (only top 10% of areas with highest travel data get flagged)
edges_filtered <- edges %>% filter(count > quantile(count, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE))
```


```{r}
#arc map large scale

#removing some noise
edges_filtered <- edges %>%
  filter(from != to) %>% # only show maps of movements from to outside of "home" cell"
  filter(count > quantile(count, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% # top 25%
  slice_max(count, n = 750) #select rows from data frame that contain the largest values of count
#calls map deck for base map
nyc_arc <-mapdeck( 
  token = Sys.getenv("MAPBOX_TOKEN"), #loads API mapbox token
  style = mapdeck_style('dark'), #chooses dark style
  pitch = 45 #pitch of map
) %>% 
  #creates the arcs
  add_animated_arc(
    data = edges_filtered, #loads data
    origin = c("from_lon", "from_lat"), # [longitude, latitude] of arc start
    destination = c("to_lon", "to_lat"), # [longitude, latitude] of arc end
    stroke_from = "#00FFCC", #sets orgin color as teal
    stroke_to = "#FF00FF", #sets destination color as pink
    stroke_from_opacity = 100, #sets stroke opacity from 0 (thick) to 255 (very thin)
    stroke_to_opacity = 100, #sets stroke opacity to 255 (very thin)
    trail_length = 0.5,#The proportion of the arc that is lit up at any one time (0 to 1)
    animation_speed = 0.2
  ) %>% 
#add shapefiles to map
 add_polygon(
    data = nyc_brgh,
    fill_opacity = 0,          # outline only
    stroke_colour = "#FFFFFF", # white border
    stroke_width = 100,         # adjust thickness
    tooltip = "BoroName"       # interactive borough names
  )
nyc_arc
```

```{r}
#save outputs
saveWidget(nyc_arc, "nyc_arc.html", selfcontained = TRUE)
saveWidget(nyc_heatmap, "nyc_heatmap.html", selfcontained = TRUE)
```
